prose: Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene computed at DFT, tier 'standard' = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. No density fitting (exact four-center integrals). Geometry: user-supplied .xyz, not re-optimized. Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). Cation state: charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion state: charge −1, multiplicity 2. All three SCFs converged; integrity status = ok, trustworthy = true. Fukui charge conservation: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f-) = 1.0000. No warnings reported. The headline value reported is the largest f+ (most electrophilic atom): atom 4 (C, ipso carbon bearing the NO2 group), f+ = 0.49641780. Full per-atom Fukui table: O(1) f+=+0.47317, f-=+0.09702, dual=+0.37616; O(2) f+=+0.47344, f-=+0.09677, dual=+0.37667; N(3) f+=-0.45670, f-=+0.01724, dual=-0.47394; C(4) f+=+0.49642, f-=-0.01429, dual=+0.51071 [MOST ELECTROPHILIC]; C(5) f+=+0.11563, f-=+0.10729 [MOST NUCLEOPHILIC], dual=+0.00834; C(6) f+=+0.11588, f-=+0.10724, dual=+0.00865; C(7) f+=+0.17412, f-=+0.09492, dual=+0.07920; C(8) f+=+0.17423, f-=+0.09489, dual=+0.07934; C(9) f+=+0.28914, f-=+0.00831, dual=+0.28082; H(10) f+=-0.23169, f-=+0.07521; H(11) f+=-0.23182, f-=+0.07520; H(12) f+=-0.13372, f-=+0.08388; H(13) f+=-0.13379, f-=+0.08388; H(14) f+=-0.12431, f-=+0.07243.
